steps.title: Track the river's change
steps.content: The narrator describes the viaduct as blocking the river at the edge of Paris. After that point, the Seine spreads out, regains space and liberty, and becomes peaceful as it moves through the countryside.

steps.title: Reject details not stated
steps.content: Choice A invents a change in water clarity. Choice B invents a stronger current under the viaduct. Choice D claims Paris surpasses the river in loveliness, which the text does not say. The remaining choice matches the city-versus-countryside contrast. The answer is C.
